data.body:
## Problem

Bulk "Move to Shipped" from the Queues screen fails with:

```
error: workflow error: invalid state transition in profile 'autopilot_no_planning': ready_for_shipment_review -> shipped
```

Only `shipment_review -> shipped` is a real transition in `work_sdlc.loom`; the only wildcards are `* -> abandoned`, `* -> blocked`, `* -> deferred`. kno correctly rejects `ready_for_shipment_review -> shipped` when `force` is not set.

## Root Cause

Commit `29311507` ("Expose Shipped transition across queue, detail, and bulk actions", 2026-04-21 10:17 +0200) introduced an illegal pattern: **Foolery TypeScript post-processes the transitions kno returns and injects synthetic `* -> <terminal>` entries**.

The offender is `withWildcardTerminals` in `src/lib/backends/knots-backend-workflows.ts:170-184`, called from `toDescriptor` at line 252. It mutates the transitions list kno provides by pushing fake `{ from: "*", to: "shipped" }` entries whenever a terminal is missing a wildcard.

`applyStateToPatch` (`src/lib/backends/knots-backend-update.ts:167-177`) then reads the same transitions list to decide whether a jump is "adjacent". It can't distinguish the fabricated `* -> shipped` from a real wildcard, so it concludes the jump is legal and omits `force: true`. kno validates against the real profile and rejects.

The same commit also added `{ from: "*", to: "shipped" }` to `canonicalTransitions()` in `src/lib/workflows.ts` — another unauthorized override of kno authority.

## Policy

Workflows defined in kno (`.loom` files) are the single source of truth. Foolery TypeScript must NEVER override, extend, augment, or post-process them. Any attempt to do so is an illegal anti-pattern.

Correction / cleaning actions (e.g. "mark this shipped now, skipping gates") must be designed descriptively as such and invoke kno idiomatically — i.e. with the `force` flag, as `KnotsBackend.close()` already does.

## Plan

1. Rip out `withWildcardTerminals` (`src/lib/backends/knots-backend-workflows.ts`). Use kno's transitions list verbatim.
2. Revert the `{ from: "*", to: "shipped" }` injection in `canonicalTransitions()` in `src/lib/workflows.ts`.
3. Delete the tests that pinned the bad behaviour:
   - `src/lib/__tests__/knots-backend-workflows-wildcard.test.ts`
   - `src/lib/__tests__/workflow-ship-wildcard.test.ts`
   - Revert the related additions in `src/lib/__tests__/valid-next-states.test.ts` and `src/lib/__tests__/beat-columns-valid-next-states.test.ts`.
4. Redesign the bulk "Ship" path as an explicit correction action: `useBulkActions` routes `shipped` / `abandoned` / `closed` targets through `BackendPort.close()` (which already forces) rather than a generic `update({ state })`. Rename/clarify the UI to reflect that this is a skip-to-terminal correction, not a normal state change.
5. Add a hard-override rule to `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `docs/DEVELOPING.md`: kno workflows are authoritative; TS must never override or extend them; correction actions must use kno idiomatically (`--force` / `force: true`).
6. Verify: reproduce the original bulk-ship flow against `autopilot_no_planning` and confirm it succeeds without the fabrication.

## Acceptance

- `withWildcardTerminals` and the `* -> shipped` canonical entry are gone; `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, and `bun run test` pass.
- Bulk "Ship" from `ready_for_shipment_review` succeeds against a real kno repo on `autopilot_no_planning`.
- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `docs/DEVELOPING.md` carry the "kno workflows are authoritative" rule.
data.title: Remove TS workflow overrides; fix ship dispatch; enshrine kno-authoritative rule
